title: Week Ahead — Market Outlook
headline: Markets enter a quieter data week but with earnings and pre-FOMC positioning as the main volatility triggers.
volatilityRationale: VIX is in a mid-teens/upper-teens range and earnings concentration plus geopolitical headlines can reprice risk quickly even with limited scheduled US macro prints.

recap.text: June CPI showed a -0.4% month-over-month decline and 3.5% year-over-year inflation, with core inflation up 2.6% over the year.
June PPI for final demand fell 0.3% month over month, with final demand goods down 1.4% and services up 0.2%.

Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivered the semiannual monetary policy report testimony to Congress in mid-July, keeping policy expectations tied to incoming inflation/labor data ahead of the July 28–29 FOMC meeting.
VIX moved higher into the upper teens by late last week, reflecting more hedging demand after a tech-led pullback.

triggers.text: Major US corporate earnings (including megacap tech) are a key test of AI-capex narratives and margins.
Fed enters its pre-meeting quiet period; the next major policy catalyst is the July 28–29 FOMC meeting and press conference.
CME options and rates expiration calendar events can influence positioning and hedging flows in interest-rate products.

watch.text: Volatility regime: VIX is at 18.31 as of Jul 20 (intraday), but the most recent FRED close available is 16.73 (Jul 16); watch whether spot vol stays bid through earnings.
Fed calendar: July’s schedule shows no listed Fed speeches/testimony for Jul 20–24, so markets may trade more on earnings, data surprises, and geopolitics.

sources.title: Reuters — futures edge higher ahead of megacap earnings (Jul 20, 2026)
